headline: Cancer Screening Rates Below 20% for Breast Cervical and Colorectal
context: National cancer screening participation remains critically low despite free programs offered at government facilities.

cards.big: Breast cervical and colorectal cancer screening rates all below 20% in 2025
cards.sub: Late-stage diagnosis accounts for 62% of cancer cases reducing survival rates

cards.big: Only 18% of eligible women had a mammogram in the past 2 years as of 2025 survey
cards.sub: Cultural barriers clinic accessibility and awareness gaps limit screening uptake

cards.big: Late-stage cancer treatment costs 4-8 times more than early-stage treatment per patient
cards.sub: Low screening rates increase both mortality and healthcare expenditure simultaneously

cards.big: South Korea achieved 72% cancer screening through employer mandate and mobile screening units
cards.sub: Workplace integration and outreach dramatically increased participation rates

cards.big: Free screening programs that only 20% use are not access programs—they are awareness failures
Should cancer screening be integrated into workplace health requirements for all employers
